title: Smoky Russian Millet Grill
description: A tender and flavorful dish featuring millet, perfect for a lazy Sunday. It's a great option for a no-onion-no-garlic-friendly meal.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to medium-high heat.
In a large bowl, mix together the millet, chicken broth, olive oil, thyme, rosemary, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lemon juice, honey, and smoked paprika to the bowl and mix until combined.
Add the chicken breast to the bowl and mix until coated with the millet mixture.
Form the mixture into patties and place them on the robata grill.
Grill the patties for 5 minutes per side, or until they're cooked through and lightly charred.
While the patties are cooking, mix together the water and honey in a small bowl.
Once the patties are done, brush them with the honey water mixture and serve hot.
Garnish with fresh herbs, if desired.
Serve the Smoky Russian Millet Grill with a side of your favorite vegetables or salad.
Let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
